parityMeta.describes: CC-1 weeklyCoachActionEngine — FUNCTION-LEVEL parity for buildWeeklyActionRecommendations (src/engines/weeklyCoachActionEngine.ts:252). Each case echoes the WeeklyCoachActionInput (always with an EXPLICIT exerciseLibrary so the default-library fallback is never hit — every row calls recommendExercisesForMuscleGap at ts:147) and the REAL engine output. Cases pin: the empty-dashboard low-confidence fallback (ts:255); the four volume-status branches (low normal high/medium ts:174, near_target/on_target low ts:195, high recovery ts:213) + deload mesocycle branch (ts:158) + pain-risk priority drop (ts:177); the technique low-high-confidence branch (ts:231 hasLowHighConfidence); adherence<70 (ts:273); heavy load-feedback (ts:290); the pain loop slice(0,2)+watch-skip with exercise vs program targetType (ts:308-325); the e1RM gap qualify/skip (ts:327-343); and the final priorityScore + targetLabel.localeCompare(_, 'zh-CN') STABLE sort + slice(0,10) (ts:345-347). The Swift WeeklyCoachActionEngine.buildWeeklyActionRecommendations re-runs the SAME builder over each echoed input and COMPUTE-ASSERTs the ordered WeeklyActionRecommendation list == golden `result`, case-by-case.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: No clock — buildWeeklyActionRecommendations is PURE (no new Date / Date.now / Math.random). Synthetic exercise ids + Chinese muscle names; no email/token/userId/deviceLabel. severityAvg values avoid exact .x5 ties so toFixed(1) is unambiguous across the JS toFixed (ties→larger) and Swift %.1f (half-to-even) implementations. Library keys are authored in ascending code-point order so the TS Object.entries iteration matches the Swift JSON-decode key order.

cases.label: empty dashboard → single low-confidence adherence fallback rec (ts:255)

comprehensive — low(+exercise text)/near_target/on_target(+technique)/high volume + adherence<70 + heavy load-feedback + pain(substitute)/watch-skip + e1RM qualify/skip → priorityScore + zh-CN localeCompare sort
deload week + low row → mesocycle rec (setsDelta 0, volumeMultiplier 0.6) instead of volume top-up (ts:158)
pain-risk on a low row → volume priority drops high→medium (ts:177) + pain program rec (no exerciseId, ts:318/320)
